creator: frank@basiskonfiguration.de
text:
Hi,

I am using FreeBSD 11.0-RELEASE-p10 with pf as a firewall for jails running on 127.0.3.0/24.

In pf.conf I configured rdr and pass for http as following:

...
rdr proto tcp from any to $ext_ip port { 80, 443 } -> 127.0.3.3
...
pass in on $ext_if proto tcp from any to 127.0.3.3 port { 80, 443 } flags S/SA keep state (max-src-conn 100, max-src-conn-rate 100/3, overload <abusive_hosts> flush)
...


In pf state output I see somtimes:

...
all tcp 127.0.3.3:80 (138.201.149.13:80) <- 178.24.68.177:55063       FIN_WAIT_2:FIN_WAIT_2
all tcp 127.0.3.3:80 (138.201.149.13:80) <- 178.24.68.177:55064       FIN_WAIT_2:FIN_WAIT_2
all tcp 127.0.3.3:80 (138.201.149.13:80) <- 178.24.68.177:55068       FIN_WAIT_2:FIN_WAIT_2
...
178.24.68.177 -> 0.0.0.0 ( states 3, connections 4294967287, rate 0.0/3s )
...


So I think that the bug is not fixed in FreeBSD 11. I had to remove "max-src-conn 100" from rule. Otherwise I have blocked IPs by mistake.
